summary: Bytes Field Guide is an eight-page Sourcey 3.6.5 documentation site for the maintained MIT-licensed Rust bytes library, grounded in 31 exact API mappings at a pinned upstream commit, published from a durable project-specific Read the Docs home, and backed by a valid 12-receipt runx Sourcey tree.

sourcey.deployment_method: Read the Docs custom build from tzwkb/bytes using the committed .readthedocs.yaml

coverage.notes: The field guide covers immutable and mutable containers, read and write cursor traits, std::io adapters, framing patterns, feature selection, capacity checks, and ownership transitions. Each inventory entry names an exact source path, line, token, category, and rendered page.

observations.evidence: Cargo.toml at pinned upstream commit d5c8ad3227afe459c09f1d0d85455abf00f0381a

observations.result: Maintained third-party repository tokio-rs/bytes under MIT
observations.evidence: Repository metadata, Cargo.toml, and LICENSE at the pinned commit

observations.result: Sourcey 3.6.5 markdown adapter with eight-page navigation
31 exact public API mappings across nine Rust source files

observations.evidence: All 11 Sourcey graph steps completed: discover, approve, author, write, build, critique, revise, rebuild, verify, and package
runx verify reported one valid tree with 12 receipts and no findings
python3 -m unittest tests.test_sourcey_docs -v, including generated assets, internal links, same-page fragments, cross-page fragments, config, pinning, and inventory
Read the Docs API build 33628289 at deployment commit c6bc06d8ad9ad1618d023bd2aad3b4c954c20952

observations.result: 8 pages and 6 core generated assets returned HTTP 200
all eight pages exposed through Sourcey navigation
observations.evidence: Live generated HTML plus sourcey.config.ts navigation contract

observations.result: dedicated project-specific Read the Docs site linked from the documentation fork
observations.evidence: bytes-field-guide.readthedocs.io builds tzwkb/bytes main through committed configuration; README identifies the site as a community supplemental field guide

gaps: The documentation is a maintained community supplement in tzwkb/bytes, not an upstream tokio-rs/bytes publication or endorsement.
The API inventory is pinned to bytes 1.12.1 and must be regenerated when upstream source lines or signatures change.

Manual Read the Docs repository configuration does not install an upstream webhook; rebuilds must be triggered from Read the Docs after future source updates unless integration permissions are expanded.
